exercises.name: Supine Chin Tuck
exercises.description: Lie on your back with knees bent. Gently tuck your chin toward your chest, making a slight double-chin. Hold briefly, then relax. Keep the back of your head on the surface throughout.
cues: Keep back of head on surface

exercises.name: Prone Pivot
exercises.description: Baby lies on tummy and pivots/rotates using arms and legs to reach for toys in a circle. Builds core strength, arm strength, and motor planning.
exercises.notes: Place toys in a circle around baby to encourage pivoting in both directions.

exercises.name: Prone on Elbows — Weight Shift
exercises.description: Baby on tummy, propped on elbows. Encourage weight shift side to side to build shoulder stability and reach.
exercises.notes: Use a toy to encourage reaching to each side while maintaining elbow prop.

exercises.name: Facilitated Rolling Back to Tummy — Right Side
exercises.description: Assist baby to roll from back to tummy leading with the right side. Encourages trunk rotation and symmetrical development.
exercises.notes: Guide from the hips or legs. Allow baby to do as much of the work as possible.

exercises.name: Facilitated Rolling Back to Tummy — Left Side
exercises.description: Assist baby to roll from back to tummy leading with the left side. Encourages trunk rotation and symmetrical development.
exercises.notes: Guide from the hips or legs. Allow baby to do as much of the work as possible.

exercises.name: Propped Sitting with Hands on Elevated Surface or Toy
exercises.description: Baby sits with hands placed on an elevated surface or toy for support. Builds sitting balance, trunk control, and upper extremity weight-bearing.
exercises.notes: Support at hips if needed. Encourage reaching with one hand while other stays on surface.

exercises.name: Lateral Head Righting — Tilting Left
exercises.description: Hold baby upright and tilt slowly to the left. Baby should right their head back to midline. Strengthens lateral neck muscles and righting reactions.
exercises.notes: Move slowly. Only tilt as far as baby can right their head. Stop if baby shows discomfort.

exercises.name: Assisted or Facilitated Side-Lying to Sitting
exercises.description: From side-lying, assist baby to push up to sitting using their arm. Builds lateral trunk strength and transitional movement skills.
exercises.notes: Guide at the shoulder and hip. Encourage baby to push through their hand.

exercises.name: Propped Sitting — To His Left Side
exercises.description: Propped sitting with support or lean to the left side to encourage weight-bearing and righting on the left. Addresses any asymmetry.
exercises.notes: Support at hips. Encourage baby to right head and trunk when weight shifted left.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squat
exercises.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Keeping your chest tall and core engaged, push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering until thighs are parallel to the floor (or as low as comfortable). Drive through your heels to return to standing.
cues: Keep chest tall, don't round forward
Drive through your heels to stand
Keep weight in mid-foot and heels
Breathe in on the way down, out on the way up

program.notes: Perform twice daily — morning and evening
